claims.text: Acme sells industrial widgets under multi-year OEM supply contracts, ~70% recurring aftermarket revenue.
Gross margin structurally supported by proprietary tooling and installed base.
The precision-widget market is mid-single-digit growth, cyclically tied to capital-goods spend.
Input costs (steel, rare-earth) are the primary industry margin swing factor.
Acme holds an estimated #2 share behind Globex; switching costs elevated by certification cycles.
Patent estate expires beginning 2031, a medium-term differentiation risk.
Revenue modeled to grow 12% then 10% on backlog conversion and price/mix.
EBIT margin expands ~200bps over the horizon on operating leverage.
New plant qualification expected 1H27 could add capacity-driven upside.
Potential aftermarket price action at the next contract reset.
Customer concentration: top 3 OEMs ~45% of revenue.
Cyclical capex downturn and input-cost inflation are the key downside risks.
Triangulated from an intrinsic DCF and a trading-comps cross-check.
Comps set anchored to two direct peers plus one adjacent-market peer.
Draft thesis: durable aftermarket franchise plus capacity-led growth is under-appreciated versus cyclical peers.
Key debate is whether operating leverage holds through a capex slowdown.
